{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[ja:READYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en}} * {{ill|Matsumoto City General Gymnasium|ja|松本市総合体育館|en}} * {{ill|Shinshu University|ja|信州大学|en}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en}}

In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30年台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en}} * {{ill|Matsumoto City General Gymnasium|ja|松本市総合体育館|en}} * {{ill|Shinshu University|ja|信州大学|en}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = [[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|thumb|Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Haiden]] | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|28.86|N|137|58|41.08|E|scale:30000|en}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en}}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|en}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|en}} to {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|en}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|en}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|en}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|ja|奥殿藩|en}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takato Domain|ja|高遠藩|en}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩|en}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|ja|信濃|en}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|en}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|en}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|en}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|en}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on [[sn:EADYFOR|READYFOR]]. The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=長野縣護國神社 |画像=[[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|250px|長野県護国神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[長野県]][[松本市]]美須々6-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神=長野県出身の国事殉難者 |社格=内務大臣指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建=[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]） |別名=美須々之宮 |例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[4月30日|30日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=長野縣
護國神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E}}}} }} '''長野縣護國神社'''（ながのけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[美須々 (松本市)|美須々]]にある[[護国神社]]である。[[明治維新]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの国難に殉じた長野県出身者を祀る。 ==歴史== 護國神社の前身となる[[招魂社]]は、[[信濃国]]では[[戊辰戦争]]の犠牲者を祭るため、[[明治]]元年（1868年）に[[松代藩]]、[[岩村田藩]]、[[須坂藩]]に建立され、その後、田口（[[奥殿藩]]）、上伊那（[[高遠藩]]）、諏訪（[[諏訪藩]]）、[[信濃招魂社|信濃]]（長野市）の4つの招魂社が建立されたが、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]の犠牲者を祭るため、[[昭和]]9年（1934年）には全県的な招魂社の造営が計画されるに至った。 昭和13年（[[1938年]]）11月、[[戊辰戦争]]で亡くなったすべての兵士のために、明治天皇により[https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii 台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください] READYFOR、'''長野県[[招魂社]]'''として仮社殿で創建された。昭和14年（[[1939年]]）3月に長野縣護國神社に改称し、同年4月1日に内務大臣指定護国神社となった。創建から2年後の昭和15年（[[1940年]]）に制定された[[松本市歌]]（作詞・[[高野辰之]]）の3番では「くにを護りの社殿は聳ゆ」と歌われている。昭和17年（[[1942年]]）、社殿・社務所・3つの鳥居などが竣工した。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後の一時期、「護国神社」の名称では軍国主義施設と見なされるということで、存続を図るために[[信濃国]]の[[枕詞]]に由来する「美須々之宮」に改称していた。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）4月、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に指定された。 昭和39年（[[1964年]]）[[5月14日]]、[[昭和天皇]]、[[香淳皇后]]が第15回[[全国植樹祭]]に合わせて県内を行幸啓。護国神社が訪問先の一つとなった{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}。 ===鳥居の倒壊=== [[2017年]]（平成29年）には[[平成29年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で第二鳥居が倒壊した。高さ7メートルの台湾ひのきによる木製で、木材を確保出来次第、設計、再建工事を行い、2019年4月頃までに完成予定とした[[クラウドファンディング]]サイト・[[Readyfor|READYFOR?]]で300万円を目標に資金調達を行ったところ、寄付総額は3,232,000円に達した。第一鳥居と脇鳥居の修理も同時に行う予定で、柱の部分だけ新調し、横木の部分は現在の木材を利用する予定だった。 [[2018年]]（平成30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で、東側にある木製の脇鳥居（高さ約6メートル）が倒壊する被害に遭った{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=[[産経新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}。これにより、補強作業に入る予定だった脇鳥居の再建も余儀なくされた。 File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|鳥居 ==周辺施設== *[[長野県松本文化会館]] *[[松本市総合体育館]] *[[信州大学]] *[[長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『長野県百科事典』 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]]、1974年 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} *[http://www.naganogokoku.jp/ 信濃國総守護 長野縣護國神社]（公式サイト） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかのけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{神社 |名称=長野縣護國神社 |画像=[[File:長野県護国神社.jpg|250px|長野県護国神社]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[長野県]][[松本市]]美須々6-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E|display=title,inline}} |祭神=長野県出身の国事殉難者 |社格=内務大臣指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建=[[昭和]]13年（[[1938年]]） |別名=美須々之宮 |例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[4月30日|30日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=長野縣
護國神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|15|28.8|N|137|58|41|E}}}} }} '''長野縣護國神社'''（ながのけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[美須々 (松本市)|美須々]]にある[[護国神社]]である。[[明治維新]]から[[第二次世界大戦]]までの国難に殉じた長野県出身者を祀る。 ==歴史== 護國神社の前身となる[[招魂社]]は、[[信濃国]]では[[戊辰戦争]]の犠牲者を祭るため、[[明治]]元年（1868年）に[[松代藩]]、[[岩村田藩]]、[[須坂藩]]に建立され、その後、田口（[[奥殿藩]]）、上伊那（[[高遠藩]]）、諏訪（[[諏訪藩]]）、[[信濃招魂社|信濃]]（長野市）の4つの招魂社が建立されたが、[[日清戦争|日清]]・[[日露戦争]]の犠牲者を祭るため、[[昭和]]9年（1934年）には全県的な招魂社の造営が計画されるに至った。 昭和13年（[[1938年]]）11月、[[戊辰戦争]]で亡くなったすべての兵士のために、明治天皇により[https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii 台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください] READYFOR、'''長野県[[招魂社]]'''として仮社殿で創建された。昭和14年（[[1939年]]）3月に長野縣護國神社に改称し、同年4月1日に内務大臣指定護国神社となった。創建から2年後の昭和15年（[[1940年]]）に制定された[[松本市歌]]（作詞・[[高野辰之]]）の3番では「くにを護りの社殿は聳ゆ」と歌われている。昭和17年（[[1942年]]）、社殿・社務所・3つの鳥居などが竣工した。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後の一時期、「護国神社」の名称では軍国主義施設と見なされるということで、存続を図るために[[信濃国]]の[[枕詞]]に由来する「美須々之宮」に改称していた。[[1957年]]（昭和32年）4月、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に指定された。 昭和39年（[[1964年]]）[[5月14日]]、[[昭和天皇]]、[[香淳皇后]]が第15回[[全国植樹祭]]に合わせて県内を行幸啓。護国神社が訪問先の一つとなった{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}。 ===鳥居の倒壊=== [[2017年]]（平成29年）には[[平成29年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で第二鳥居が倒壊した。高さ7メートルの台湾ひのきによる木製で、木材を確保出来次第、設計、再建工事を行い、2019年4月頃までに完成予定とした[[クラウドファンディング]]サイト・[[Readyfor|READYFOR?]]で300万円を目標に資金調達を行ったところ、寄付総額は3,232,000円に達した。第一鳥居と脇鳥居の修理も同時に行う予定で、柱の部分だけ新調し、横木の部分は現在の木材を利用する予定だった。 [[2018年]]（平成30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で、東側にある木製の脇鳥居（高さ約6メートル）が倒壊する被害に遭った{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=[[産経新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}。これにより、補強作業に入る予定だった脇鳥居の再建も余儀なくされた。 File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|鳥居 ==周辺施設== *[[長野県松本文化会館]] *[[松本市総合体育館]] *[[信州大学]] *[[長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校]] == 関連項目 == * [[松本市#名所・旧跡|松本市の名所・旧跡]] * [[松本市の歴史]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『長野県百科事典』 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]]、1974年 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} *[http://www.naganogokoku.jp/ 信濃國総守護 長野縣護國神社]（公式サイト） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかのけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|qid=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654335}} * [[:en:Matsumoto City Gymnasium|Matsumoto City General Gymnasium]] * [[:en:Shinshu University|Shinshu University]] * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|qq=|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654341}}

'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.

In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>

After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>

The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].

These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).

It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] to [[:en:World War II|World War II]].
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] to honor the war dead from the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ([[:en:Okutono Domain|Okutono Domain]]), Kamiina ([[:en:Takatō Domain|Takato Domain]]), Suwa ([[:en:Suwa Domain|Suwa Domain]]), and in [[:en:Shinano|Shinano]] (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]] and [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three [[:en:Torii|Torii]] gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]].
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during [[:en:Typhoon Lan (2017)|Typhoon Lan]]. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
