It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|en|Okutono Domain|WD=Q3034400|lt=Okutono Domain}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takatō Domain|en|Takatō Domain|WD=Q988499|lt=Takato Domain}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|WD=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|en|Shinano|WD=Q255030|lt=Shinano}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Lan (2017)|en|Typhoon Lan (2017)|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon Lan}}. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:長野縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11654831]] Wikidata name: Naganoken-Gokoku-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|en|Okutono Domain|WD=Q3034400|lt=Okutono Domain}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takatō Domain|en|Takatō Domain|WD=Q988499|lt=Takato Domain}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|WD=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|en|Shinano|WD=Q255030|lt=Shinano}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Lan (2017)|en|Typhoon Lan (2017)|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon Lan}}. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654335}} * {{ill|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|en|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|WD=Q11531051|lt=Matsumoto City General Gymnasium}} * {{ill|Shinshu University|en|Shinshu University|WD=Q1209029|lt=Shinshu University}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654341}}
== Notes == {{Reflist|en}}
== References == * {{ill|Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県百科事典|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}, 1974
== External Links == {{Commonscat|en}} * [http://www.naganogokoku.jp/ Official website of Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shokonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|en|Okutono Domain|WD=Q3034400|lt=Okutono Domain}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takatō Domain|en|Takatō Domain|WD=Q988499|lt=Takato Domain}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|WD=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|en|Shinano|WD=Q255030|lt=Shinano}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Lan (2017)|en|Typhoon Lan (2017)|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon Lan}}. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654335}} * {{ill|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|en|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|WD=Q11531051|lt=Matsumoto City General Gymnasium}} * {{ill|Shinshu University|en|Shinshu University|WD=Q1209029|lt=Shinshu University}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654341}}
== Notes == {{Reflist|en}}
== References == * {{ill|Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県百科事典|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}, 1974
== External Links == {{Commonscat|en}} * [http://www.naganogokoku.jp/ Official website of Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|en|Okutono Domain|WD=Q3034400|lt=Okutono Domain|1=Okutono Domain}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takatō Domain|en|Takatō Domain|WD=Q988499|lt=Takato Domain|1=Takatō Domain}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|WD=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain|1=Suwa Domain}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|en|Shinano|WD=Q255030|lt=Shinano|1=Shinano}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Lan (2017)|en|Typhoon Lan (2017)|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon Lan|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image2 = File:Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine.jpg | image = File:長野県護国神社.jpg | location = Misuzu 6-1, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36.258017|N|137.978078|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Patriots from Nagano Prefecture | type = Interior Minister Designated Gokoku Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1938 (Shōwa 13) | alternate_name = Misuzu no Miya | festival = April 29 and April 30 |en| prefecture= {{ill|Matsumoto|WD=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} }}
'''Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (長野縣護國神社, Nagano-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} located in Misuzu, Matsumoto City, Nagano Prefecture. It enshrines those from Nagano Prefecture who perished in national crises from the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} to {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.
== History == The forerunner of the Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810}}, was established in various domains within {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} to honor the war dead from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. These were set up in the Matsushiro, Iwamurada, and Suzaka domains in 1868 (Meiji 1). Later, additional Shōkonsha were established in Taguchi ({{ill|Okutono Domain|en|Okutono Domain|WD=Q3034400|lt=Okutono Domain|1=Okutono Domain}}), Kamiina ({{ill|Takatō Domain|en|Takatō Domain|WD=Q988499|lt=Takato Domain|1=Takatō Domain}}), Suwa ({{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|WD=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain|1=Suwa Domain}}), and in {{ill|Shinano|en|Shinano|WD=Q255030|lt=Shinano|1=Shinano}} (Nagano City). To commemorate those who perished in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}} and {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, plans for a prefecture-wide Shōkonsha were made in 1934 (Shōwa 9).
In November 1938 (Shōwa 13), the Nagano Prefecture Shōkonsha was established as a temporary shrine by order of Emperor Meiji to honor all soldiers who died in the Boshin War<ref name="cloud">{{cite web |url=https://readyfor.jp/projects/torii |title=台風で倒壊した長野縣護國神社の鳥居再建にお力を貸してください |work=READYFOR |accessdate=2023-06-28|en}}</ref>. It was renamed Nagano Prefecture Gokoku Shrine in March 1939 (Shōwa 14) and designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs on April 1 of the same year. The third verse of the Matsumoto City song, established two years after its founding in 1940 (Shōwa 15), mentions the shrine: "The shrine stands to protect the country." In 1942 (Shōwa 17), the shrine buildings, office, and three {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} gates were completed<ref name="cloud" />.
After World War II, to avoid being perceived as a militaristic facility, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Misuzu no Miya," derived from the ancient name for Shinano Province. In April 1957 (Shōwa 32), it was designated a Beppyo Shrine by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
On May 14, 1964 (Shōwa 39), Emperor Shōwa and Empress Kōjun visited the shrine during their inspection of the prefecture for the 15th National Arbor Day<ref>{{cite book |author=Hara Takeshi |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=126 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4|en}}</ref>.
=== Torii Collapse === In 2017 (Heisei 29), the second torii gate collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Lan (2017)|en|Typhoon Lan (2017)|WD=Q42108577|lt=Typhoon Lan|1=Typhoon Lan (2017)}}. Standing 7 meters tall and made of Taiwanese cypress, the gate's reconstruction was funded through a crowdfunding campaign on . The campaign raised ¥3,232,000, surpassing the ¥3 million goal<ref name="cloud" />. Repairs to the first torii and side torii gates were also planned, with new pillars and reused crosspieces.
In 2018 (Heisei 30), the side torii gate on the east side, also made of wood and about 6 meters tall, collapsed during {{ill|Typhoon Jebi|ja|平成30th year 台風第21号|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Jebi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20180905-NWSBDZ63N5LPZOUISKZE7UA7Y4/ |title=長野県護国神社で鳥居がまた倒壊 |work=産経新聞 |accessdate=2018-09-07|en}}</ref>. This required an unscheduled rebuild.
<gallery> File:長野縣護國神社 Naganokenn Gokoku jinja - panoramio.jpg|Torii </gallery>
== Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|ja|長野県松本文化会館|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|1=Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Culture Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654335}} * {{ill|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|en|Matsumoto City Gymnasium|WD=Q11531051|lt=Matsumoto City General Gymnasium|1=Matsumoto City Gymnasium}} * {{ill|Shinshu University|en|Shinshu University|WD=Q1209029|lt=Shinshu University|1=Shinshu University}} * {{ill|Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|ja|長野県松本美須々ヶ丘高等学校|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural Matsumoto Misuzu High School|1=Matsumoto Misuzugaoka High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11654341}}
== Notes == {{Reflist|en}}
== References == * {{ill|Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県百科事典|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encyclopedia of Nagano Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}, 1974
